name: cfchecker

metadata.name: cfchecker
metadata.summary: The NetCDF Climate Forecast Conventions compliance checker

documentation:
# CF Checker

The CF Checker is a utility that checks the contents of a NetCDF file complies with the Climate and Forecasts (CF) Metadata Convention.

## Dependencies

* The package runs on [**Linux**](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linux)
  and [**Mac OS**](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mac_OS) operating systems.

* [Python 3.x](https://www.python.org/)

* [netcdf4-python](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/netCDF4) at version 1.2.5 or newer. This package requires [netCDF](https://www.unidata.ucar.edu/software/netcdf/), [HDF5](https://www.hdfgroup.org/solutions/hdf5/) and [zlib](ftp://ftp.unidata.ucar.edu/pub/netcdf/netcdf-4) libraries.

* [cfunits-python](https://bitbucket.org/cfpython/cfunits-python) package version 3.0.0 or newer

* [numpy](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/numpy) version 1.15 or newer

## Installation

To install from [PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/cfchecker):

    pip install cfchecker

Alternatively, to install from source:

1. Download the cfchecker package from [cfchecker releases](https://github.com/cedadev/cf-checker/releases)

2. Unpack the library:

        tar -zxf cfchecker-${version}.tar.gz

        cd cfchecker-${version}

3. Install the package:

   * To install to a central location:

            python setup.py install

   * To install to a non standard location:

            python setup.py install --prefix=<directory>

     If directory you are installing into is not on PYTHONPATH you will need to add it.

## Running the CF Checker

`cfchecks [-a <area-types.xml>] [-r <regions.xml>] [-s <std_names.xml>] [-v <CFVersion>] [-x] [-t <cache_time_days>] file1 [file2...]`

For further details and for other available command line options please see the help by running `cfchecks -h`

### Environment Variables

The following parameters can be set on the command-line or through environment variables:

1. `CF_STANDARD_NAMES` or (CL option `-s`) : The path or URL to the CF standard names table
2. `CF_AREA_TYPES` or (CL option `-a`) : The path or URL to the CF area types table
3. `CF_REGION_NAMES` or (CL option `-r`): The path or URL to the CF region names table


### Running the Test script

In the release tarball there is a `test_files` directory containing a `test.sh` script which runs a series of test files through the CF Checker and confirms the checker is working as expected.  It is a very elementary system, which will be rewritten soon.  Before running it you will need to edit the location of the cfchecks script in the `tests.sh` file:

    cfchecker="<location of cfchecks>"

Then just run the `tests.sh` script:

    ./tests.sh

modules.name: cfchecker.cfchecks
documentation:
cfchecks [OPTIONS] file1 [file2...]

Description:
 The CF Checker checks NetCDF files for compliance to the CF standard.
 
Options:
 -a or --area_types:
       the location of the CF area types table (xml)
       
 -h or --help: Prints this help text

 -r or --region_names:
       the location of the CF standardized region names table (xml)

 -s or --cf_standard_names:
       the location of the CF standard name table (xml)

 -t or --cache_time_days <days>:
       set the cache retention period in days [default 10 days].

 -v or --version: 
       CF version to check against, use auto to auto-detect the file version.

 -x or --cache_tables:
       cache the standard name, area type and region name tables.

 --cache_dir:
       directory in which to store cached tables
!/usr/bin/env python
Name: cfchecks.py
Author: Rosalyn Hatcher - Met Office, UK
Maintainer: Rosalyn Hatcher - NCAS-CMS, Univ. of Reading, UK
Date: February 2003
File Revision: $Revision: 200 $
CF Checker Version: See __version__

functions.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.normalize_whitespace
documentation: Remove redundant whitespace from a string.

functions.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.isnt_str_or_basestring
documentation:
check if the passed in thing is a str, or if running under python 2,
a basestring

functions.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.is_str_or_basestring
documentation:
check if the passed in thing is a str, or if running under python 2,
a basestring

functions.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.check_derived_name
documentation:
Checks whether name is a derived standard name and adheres
to the transformation rules. See CF standard names document
for more information.

functions.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.getargs
documentation: getargs(arglist): parse command line options and environment variables

classes.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFVersion

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFVersion.__init__
documentation: Instantiate CFVersion with a string or with a tuple of ints

A CF version number, stored as a tuple, that can be instantiated with 
a tuple or a string, written out as a string, and compared with another version

classes.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.ConstructDict
documentation:
Parse the xml standard_name table, reading all entries into a dictionary;
storing standard_name and units.

If useShelve is True, a python shelve file will be used. If the file is
present and less than 600 seconds old, the existing contents will be used,
otherwise the standard name table will be parsed and written to the shelf 
file.

classes.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.ConstructList
documentation:
Parse the xml area_type table, reading all area_types 
into a list.

classes.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._init_results
documentation:
Add a results dictionary to all_results, and for the time being point 
self.results at it, so that _add_error() and other methods will update 
results for the current file

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._fatal
documentation:
Add a fatal error message.  Arguments are as per _add_error below, and the error message 
is therefore stored, but primarily a fatal error message aborts the checking of the file,
raising a FatalCheckerError.  The caller can of course trap this and not exit.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._add_error
documentation:
Add an error message to the output 
(for the filename for which _init_results() was most recently called)
This will cause the message to be stored in self.all_results and self.all_messages,
and also printed (unless instantiated with silent=True)

Usage:  self._add_error(message) - adds an error in global section
        self._add_error(message, varname) - adds an error in variables section

optional argument code='...' specifies the error code e.g. '1.1.1'

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._add_warn
documentation: "as _add_error but for warnings

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._add_info
documentation: as _add_error but for informational messages

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._add_version
documentation: as _add_error but for informational messages

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._add_debug
documentation:
As _add_error but for debug messages. Does nothing unless debug has been set to True,
e.g. by adding the -d command-line flag if instantiating via main()

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._add_message
documentation: add the message - generic helper for _add_error etc.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._join_strings
documentation: filter out None from lists and join the rest

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.get_total_counts
documentation: Get counts totalled over all files checked.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.get_counts
documentation:
get OrderedDict of number of errors, warnings, info messages
This will be for most recently checked file, unless 'results' is passed in 
(in which case, it should be a value from the self.all_results dictionary
where corresponding key is the filename).

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._update_counts
documentation: helper for _get_counts()

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker._checker
documentation: Main implementation of checker assuming self.f exists.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.setUpAttributeList
documentation:
Set up Dictionary of valid attributes, their corresponding
Type; S(tring), N(umeric) D(ata variable type)  and Use C(oordinate),
D(ata non-coordinate) or G(lobal) variable.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.uniqueList
documentation: Determine if list has any repeated elements.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.isNumeric
documentation: Determine if variable is of Numeric data type.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.isTime
documentation:
Variable is a time axis coordinate if it has one or more of the following:
1) The axis attribute has the value 'T'
2) Units of reference time
3) The standard_name attribute is one of 'time' or 'forecast_reference_time'

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.getStdName
documentation: Get standard_name of variable.  Return it as 2 parts - the standard name and the modifier, if present.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.getInterpretation
documentation:
Determine the interpretation (time - T, height or depth - Z,
latitude - Y or longitude - X) of a dimension.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.getCoordinateDataVars
documentation:
Obtain list of coordinate data variables, boundary
variables, climatology variables and grid_mapping variables.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.unique
documentation: Get a unique values from list

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.subst
documentation: substitute tokens for WORD and SEP (space or end of string)

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.get_variable_attributes
documentation: Get all attributes of this variable and store in a dictionary

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkGeometryContainerVar
documentation: Section 7.5: Geometry Container Variable Checks

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkNodesAttribute
documentation: Validate nodes attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkGridMappingAttribute
documentation: Validate syntax of grid_mapping attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.validGridMappingAttributes
documentation: Setup dictionary of valid grid mapping attributes and their types

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkGridMappingVar
documentation: Section 5.6: Grid Mapping Variable Checks

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.setUpFormulas
documentation: Set up dictionary of all valid formulas

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.parseBlankSeparatedList
documentation: Parse blank separated list

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.extendedBlankSeparatedList
documentation:
Check list is a blank separated list of words containing alphanumeric characters
plus underscore '_', period '.', plus '+', hyphen '-', or "at" sign '@'.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.commaOrBlankSeparatedList
documentation:
Check list is a blank or comma separated list of words containing alphanumeric
characters plus underscore '_', period '.', plus '+', hyphen '-', or "at" sign '@'.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkGlobalAttributes
documentation: Check validity of global attributes.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.getFileCFVersion
documentation:
Return CF version of file, used for auto version option. If Conventions is COARDS return CF-1.0,
else a valid version based on Conventions else an empty version (for auto version)

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.validName
documentation:
Check for valid name.  They should begin with a
letter and be composed of letters, digits and underscores.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkDimensions
documentation:
Check variable has non-repeated dimensions, that space/time dimensions are listed in the order T,Z,Y,X
and that any non space/time dimensions are added to the left of the space/time dimensions, unless it
is a boundary variable or climatology variable, where 1 trailing dimension is allowed.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.getTypeCode
documentation: Get the type, as a 1-character code

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkAttribute
documentation:
Check the syntax of the attribute name, that the attribute
is of the correct type and that it is attached to the right
kind of variable.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkCFRole
documentation: Validate cf_role attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkRaggedArray
documentation: Validate count/index variable

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.isValidCellMethodTypeValue
documentation:
Is <type1> or <type2> in the cell_methods attribute a valid value
(method may have side-effect of logging additional errors,
and for this purpose the variable name is also passed in)

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkCellMethods
documentation:
Checks on cell_methods attribute
dim1: [dim2: [dim3: ...]] method [where type1 [over type2]] [ (comment) ]
where comment is of the form:  ([interval: value unit [interval: ...] comment:] remainder)

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkCellMeasures
documentation:
Checks on cell_measures attribute:
1) Correct syntax
2) Reference valid variable
3) Valid measure

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkFormulaTerms
documentation:
Checks on formula_terms attribute (CF Section 4.3.3):
formula_terms = var: term var: term ...
1) No standard_name present
2) No formula defined for std_name
3) Invalid formula_terms syntax
4) Var referenced, not declared

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkUnits
documentation: Check units attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkBADCUnits
documentation: Check units allowed by BADC

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkValidMinMaxRange
documentation: Check that valid_range and valid_min/valid_max are not both specified

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkComputedStandardName
documentation: Check if var computed_standard_name attribute that it also has formula_terms attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkActualRange
documentation:
Check that the actual_range:
1) is the same type as its associated variable or scale_factor/add_offset if set
2) has 2 elements where the first equals the min non-missing value and the second the max
after any scale_factor/add_offset applied
3) is not present if all data values are equal to missing value
4) is valid if valid_range/valid_min/valid_max are specified

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chk_FillValue
documentation:
Check 1) type of _FillValue
2) _FillValue lies outside of valid_range
3) type of missing_value

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkAxisAttribute
documentation: Check validity of axis attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkDescription
documentation:
Check 1) standard_name & long_name attributes are present
2) for a valid standard_name as listed in the standard name table.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.getStringValue
documentation:
Collapse (by concatenation) the outermost (fastest varying) dimension of string valued array into
memory. E.g. [['a','b','c']] becomes ['abc']

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkCompressAttr
documentation: Check Compress Attribute

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.equalNumOfValues
documentation: Check that arg1 and arg2 contain the same number elements.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkMultiDimCoord
documentation:
If a coordinate variable is multi-dimensional, then it is recommended
that the variable name should not match the name of any of its dimensions.

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.chkValuesMonotonic
documentation:
A coordinate variable must have values that are strictly monotonic
(increasing or decreasing).

methods.name: cfchecker.cfchecks.CFChecker.isStrictlyMonotonic
documentation: Is array strictly monotonic increasing or decreasing
Checking class
